expectations.text: generates-observability-scripts: At least 2 observability-related scripts are generated
expectations.evidence: 9 files generated: 7 TypeScript modules (01-structured-logger.ts at 4,313 bytes, 02-metrics-collector.ts at 8,862 bytes, 03-health-checker.ts at 12,357 bytes, 04-session-tracer.ts at 10,640 bytes, 05-telemetry-pipeline-monitor.ts at 10,445 bytes, 06-analytics-event-inspector.ts at 7,802 bytes, 07-observability-integration.ts at 11,114 bytes), 1 shell script (observability-agent.sh at 15,282 bytes, executable), and 1 README (4,778 bytes). Well above the 2-script minimum.

expectations.text: scripts-are-executable-logic: Scripts contain actual executable logic, not just placeholder comments
expectations.evidence: All files contain fully implemented logic: TypeScript files export working classes/functions (Counter, Gauge, Histogram with real math including percentile calculations, Map-based storage with eviction logic), health check functions with real process.memoryUsage()/process.cpuUsage() calls, HTTP connectivity checks with AbortController timeouts, span lifecycle management with TTL-based eviction, telemetry pipeline monitoring with stall/degradation detection, and analytics event validation with PII detection regex patterns. The shell script (observability-agent.sh) has 7 subcommands (health/telemetry/traces/session/env/diagnose/watch) with real jq parsing, curl connectivity checks, fswatch monitoring, and process memory inspection. No placeholder comments detected.

expectations.text: references-project-structure: Scripts reference the actual project structure
expectations.evidence: Extensive references to actual project structure throughout: src/utils/telemetry/instrumentation.ts (MeterProvider, PeriodicExportingMetricReader), src/utils/telemetry/bigqueryExporter.ts (BigQueryMetricsExporter), src/utils/telemetry/sessionTracing.ts (SPAN_TTL_MS, span types), src/utils/telemetry/perfettoTracing.ts (trace file writes, evictOldestEvents), src/utils/telemetry/logger.ts (ClaudeCodeDiagLogger), src/services/analytics/datadog.ts (DATADOG_ALLOWED_EVENTS, trackDatadogEvent, tengu_* event names), src/services/analytics/firstPartyEventLogger.ts (batch config), src/services/analytics/sink.ts, src/utils/auth.ts, src/utils/config.ts. The README contains a full alignment table mapping project sources to generated scripts.

expectations.text: assesses-observability-dimensions: Output includes assessment of O1-O4 observability dimensions
expectations.evidence: All four observability dimensions are covered: O1 (Logging) - 01-structured-logger.ts provides leveled structured logging with JSON output, trace correlation, child loggers, and session-aware factory; O2 (Metrics) - 02-metrics-collector.ts implements counters/histograms/gauges with CLI-specific helpers for API calls (including TTFT, cache tokens), tool execution, permissions, and spans; O3 (Tracing) - 04-session-tracer.ts mirrors the project's span types (interaction, llm_request, tool, tool.blocked_on_user, tool.execution, hook) with parent-child hierarchy, TTL-based eviction, and trace export; O4 (Health) - 03-health-checker.ts runs 8 parallel health checks (memory, event loop lag, disk space, API connectivity, auth state, telemetry pipeline, trace file writes, Node.js version). Additionally, 05-telemetry-pipeline-monitor.ts monitors all 7 telemetry pipelines (otlp_metrics, otlp_logs, otlp_traces, bigquery, datadog, first_party, perfetto) for stalls and degradation, and 07-observability-integration.ts provides a unified diagnose() method that aggregates all dimensions into a single DiagnosticReport with recommendations. No explicit O1-O4 labels, but comprehensive coverage of all dimensions.
